name: The Fifth Lake Trail: Senterada to Lake Montcortès
name_original: Running - Lago de Montcortés desde Senterada — Pallars Sobirà
description: This difficult 8.3-mile (13.4 km) jogging route from Senterada features significant elevation gain and the unique karstic Lake Montcortés.
region_intro: Jogging around Senterada offers a diverse landscape for runners. The region is characterized by river valleys, such as those near la Pobla de Segur, and features notable natural landmarks like Lake Montcortès. Trails often include varied terrain, from relatively flat sections along water bodies to more challenging ascents with significant elevation gains, reflecting the area's mountainous surroundings. Best jogging routes around Senterada The most popular jogging route is The Fifth Lake Trail: Senterada to Lake Montcortès, a 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 53 minutes to complete. This route features a substantial elevation gain, leading runners through varied terrain to a scenic lake. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from la Pobla de Segur, a moderate 4.4 miles (7.0 km) path. This loop offers a relatively flat profile, making it suitable for a steady run through the local area. Local runners also love the Rutilla loop from la Pobla de Segur, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail leading through the surrounding hills, often completed in about 1 hour 15 minutes. Jogging around Senterada is defined by river valleys, lake views, and rolling hills. The network of running routes offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging trails with significant climbs. The routes in Senterada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 170 runners have used komoot to explore Senterada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Senterada?
A: Senterada offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 35 trails to explore. These range from easy loops to more challenging mountain runs, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Senterada?
A: The running trails in Senterada are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 5 reviews. Runners frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from river valleys to scenic lake views, and the variety of terrain available.

Q: When is the best time of year for running in Senterada?
A: The best time for running in Senterada is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are mild and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, especially in the mornings or evenings, though some higher elevation routes might be more exposed to sun. Winter offers unique opportunities for trail running, but some higher or more remote paths may be affected by snow or ice.

Q: Are there options for winter jogging in Senterada?
A: Yes, winter jogging is possible in Senterada, particularly on lower elevation routes or those along river valleys. However, trails at higher altitudes or in more shaded areas might be covered in snow or ice, requiring appropriate gear and caution. Always check local conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in Senterada?
A: Absolutely. Senterada has at least 1 easy running route perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed pace. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and smoother terrain. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31857482" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from la Pobla de Segur&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a relatively flat profile.

Q: Where can I find more challenging running trails near Senterada?
A: For runners seeking a challenge, Senterada offers over 25 difficult running routes with significant elevation gains. A notable option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931144301/the-fifth-lake-trail-senterada-to-lake-montcortes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Fifth Lake Trail: Senterada to Lake Montcortès&lt;/a&gt;, which features substantial climbs and varied terrain leading to a scenic lake.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Senterada area?
A: Yes, Senterada features several excellent circular running routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rutilla loop from la Pobla de Segur&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, leading through surrounding hills. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24552552" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pobla de Segur – Claverol Castle loop from la Pobla de Segur&lt;/a&gt;, offering a moderate challenge.

Q: Are the running trails around Senterada dog-friendly?
A: Many of the running trails in the Senterada region are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy your run with your canine companion. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife. Check specific route details on komoot for any restrictions.

Q: Can I find family-friendly jogging routes in Senterada?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, routes with lower difficulty grades and shorter distances are generally suitable for families. The easier sections along river valleys or around Lake Montcortès could be good options for a family jog. Always consider the distance and elevation profile to match your family's capabilities.

Q: Are there any running routes with places to eat or cafes nearby?
A: Many routes in the Senterada area start or pass through villages like la Pobla de Segur, where you can find cafes and restaurants. For example, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31857482" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from la Pobla de Segur&lt;/a&gt; offer easy access to amenities before or after your run.

Q: What are some hidden gem running trails in Senterada?
A: While specific 'hidden gems' are often discovered by local exploration, the less-trafficked trails leading into the hills surrounding Senterada can offer a sense of discovery. Look for routes that venture away from main roads and explore the smaller valleys and ridges for a unique experience. The komoot community often shares insights on these lesser-known paths.

Q: Is parking available for the running trails in Senterada?
A: Parking is generally available in the towns and villages where many of the running routes begin, such as Senterada itself or la Pobla de Segur. Specific parking information can often be found in the route descriptions on komoot, or by checking local signage upon arrival.

Q: How accessible are Senterada's running routes by public transport?
A: Public transport access to Senterada and surrounding villages is available, though it might be limited compared to larger urban areas. Buses connect Senterada to larger towns like la Pobla de Segur. Routes starting directly from these villages, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152731" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devil's Bridge loop from Senterada&lt;/a&gt;, are often the most convenient for public transport users.

Q: What can I see along the jogging routes in Senterada?
A: The running routes in Senterada offer diverse sights. You can encounter natural landmarks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2472984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Montcortés&lt;/a&gt;, as seen on 'The Fifth Lake Trail'. Other routes might lead you past historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5164268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salàs de Pallars Historic Village&lt;/a&gt; or offer views of various mountain passes and natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2438181" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de la O&lt;/a&gt;.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Running - Lake Montcortés from Senterada trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as difficult. It is a challenging route due to its length of approximately 13.4 km and significant elevation gain of around 468 meters. It requires good physical fitness and sure-footedness, though it primarily follows well-marked paths and tracks without highly technical sections.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this running route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You will encounter alternating sections of established trails (senderos/corriols) and wider tracks (pistas). Expect a semi-asphalted track, a stony path descending a ravine, and areas with eroded calcareous terrain. The route also leads through diverse landscapes including forests of oaks, red pines, and boxwoods, as well as open meadows.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Running - Lake Montcortés from Senterada trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Senterada. While specific parking lots are not detailed, you should be able to find parking within the village of Senterada to access the trailhead.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the trailhead in Senterada?
faqs.answer: Information regarding direct public transport options to Senterada for accessing this specific trailhead is limited in the provided sources. It is advisable to check local bus schedules or regional transport services for connections to Pallars Sobirà if you plan to rely on public transport.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Running - Lake Montcortés from Senterada trail is a circular route, meaning you will end up back at your starting point in Senterada after completing the run.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: A primary highlight of this route is Lake Montcortés, a unique karstic lake fed by underground waters. You will also pass through charming rural mountain villages like Mentui and la Pobleta de Bellveí, and enjoy picturesque scenery characteristic of the Pallars Sobirà region, including high mountains, lush nature, and diverse forests.

faqs.question: What makes Lake Montcortés unique?
faqs.answer: Lake Montcortés is geologically unique because it is a karstic lake, sustained by subterranean springs, rather than being of glacial origin like most other lakes in the Pyrenees. This makes it a natural marvel and a significant stop for migratory birds.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, a portion of this trail coincides with the "El Cinquè Llac. Etapa 5 : Senterada => La Pobla de Segur" and "El Cinquè Llac. Etapa 4 : Les Esglésies => Senterada" routes, which are part of the well-known multi-stage hiking traverse, "Cinquè Llac." It also shares sections with "El Camí IT1TR6 El Camí Capçaler: de Salzes a Balaguer" and "TransCatalunya. Catalunya Central - Pirineus".

faqs.question: Is the Running - Lake Montcortés from Senterada trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, specific rules regarding dogs are not detailed in the provided information. It is generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users, especially in areas with livestock or protected species. Please practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to run this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no mention of any required permits or entrance fees for accessing the Running - Lake Montcortés from Senterada trail. It appears to be freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during various seasons, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant running conditions with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, so an early start is recommended. Winter running is possible but may involve colder temperatures and potentially snow or ice, especially at higher points, requiring appropriate gear.
